message:
feat: add ftui theme integration, console UX, and config enhancements (br-1m6a)

- Add theme.rs module with ftui-extras theme integration (br-1m6a.12)
  - CyberpunkAurora/Darcula/LumenLight/NordicFrost/HighContrast support
  - Semantic color functions: primary, secondary, accent, success, warning, error
  - Sparkline gradient and JSON syntax highlighting colors
- Replace all hardcoded ANSI constants in console.rs with theme-derived colors
- Add console.rs rich output module: startup banner, tool call panels,
  sparkline buffer, toast notifications, log panels, HTTP request panels
- Add secret masking in tool call params/results (br-1m6a.18)
- Add ANSI/OSC stripping test helpers + console integration tests (br-1m6a.14)
- Add PTY console E2E suite (br-1m6a.15)
- Extend Config with console layout, persistence, and HTTP logging fields
- Wire theme init into both stdio and HTTP server entry points
- Fix HTTP request panel test assertions for new box-drawing format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm.8","title":"Bearer Auth Parity","description":"Objective:
Ensure Bearer token auth middleware behavior matches legacy Python, including localhost bypass logic and forwarded-header safeguards.

Legacy Reference Behavior:
- If bearer token configured, require Authorization: Bearer <token>
- If allow_localhost_unauthenticated and client is localhost (no forwarded headers), bypass Authorization
- OPTIONS and /health/* always allowed
- Constant-time comparison for token equality

Implementation Plan:
- Verify Rust middleware path covers OPTIONS + /health/* bypass.
- Enforce constant-time token compare.
- Use peer_addr + forwarded headers for localhost detection (`br-1bm.3`).

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for token comparison + bypass conditions.
- Integration tests for /health endpoints, OPTIONS, and standard POSTs.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.6` (HTTP parity suite) including forwarded-header negative cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Responses and bypass behavior match legacy exactly (status + JSON body).
- Constant-time compare is used (no early-return string compare).
- E2E artifacts include explicit allow/deny traces and header diffs on mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:03:49.815525779Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:03:49.815475775Z","close_reason":"Implemented legacy bearer auth parity + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:23.276913856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.484645140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.9","title":"Health + Well-Known Endpoints Parity Tests","description":"Objective:

 {"id":"br-1e5h","title":"DB concurrency parity: backoff + circuit breaker","description":"## Objective
Implement database lock contention handling and circuit breaker per legacy concurrency model.

## Scope
- Exponential backoff with jitter on SQLITE_BUSY/locked errors.
- Circuit breaker: after 5 consecutive failures, open for 30s (fail fast with `DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED`/`RESOURCE_BUSY`).
- Connection pooling defaults: base 3 + overflow 4, recycle 30min, busy timeout 5s.

## Tests
- Unit tests simulating lock contention and backoff sequence.
- Integration tests for circuit breaker open/close behavior.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store backoff/circuit logs under `tests/artifacts/db/concurrency/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Backoff and circuit breaker match legacy thresholds and timings.
2. Errors are mapped to legacy error codes.
3. Tests are deterministic and do not rely on real contention.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:50.675607847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705741026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705722471Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1e5h","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:55.369503695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-1m6a","title":"[epic] Dramatically enhance console output with frankentui","description":"Master epic for dramatically improving the MCP Agent Mail server console UX using FrankenTUI.

BACKGROUND
- Legacy Python has rich_logger.py: highly structured Rich panels for startup, tool calls, and key events.
- Rust server already uses FrankenTUI inline mode (TerminalWriter) for a persistent HUD while preserving scrollback.

PRIMARY GOALS
- Python parity: replicate the important *information architecture* of rich_logger.py (startup banner sections, tool call start/end panels, query stats, request panels).
- FrankenTUI-native upgrades: keep a stable inline HUD, add sparklines, toast notifications, subtle animations, and better discoverability (links, tips, command palette) without sacrificing determinism.
- User-tunable console: interactive layout configuration (bottom % vs left split) and theme selection, persisted to a user config file so it "just remembers" (enabled by default in real TTYs).
- Safety by default: never leak secrets in panels; mask sensitive values; keep output bounded (truncate huge JSON).
- Zero-surprise behavior: when rich output is disabled or stdout is not a TTY, fall back to plain, grep-friendly text with no giant ANSI art dumps.
- Performance: when tool logging is disabled, overhead must be near-zero (no JSON pretty-printing, no allocations on hot path).

SCOPE
- Server console only (primarily `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server`).
- Includes both HTTP mode and stdio mode behavior where it impacts console output.

KEY CONSTRAINTS
- Inline mode must preserve scrollback and not corrupt the HUD.
- Output must remain deterministic enough for unit tests and E2E scripts.

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION (PREFERRED)
- Prefer rendering rich panels via ftui widgets into an offscreen `ftui::Buffer` + `ftui::Frame`, then exporting to ANSI using `ftui-extras` (TextExporter). This avoids brittle manual width/ANSI bookkeeping.
- Centralize styling on FrankenTUI's theme system (no ad-hoc ANSI constants).
- Centralize masking + safe JSON formatting utilities.

DONE WHEN
- Startup banner matches parity sections and is pleasant in real terminals.
- Tool call start/end panels exist with syntax-highlighted JSON and per-call query stats.
- Request panels are color-coded and consistent with theme.
- HUD shows useful live telemetry (counts, req/s trends, toasts).
- Unit tests + new console E2E suite pass and are stable in CI.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:51:17.164232623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:00:43.103293281Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-1m6a.1","title":"Rich ASCII art startup banner with full Python parity","description":"Upgrade the startup banner to be FrankenTUI-native and Python-parity-aligned.

CURRENT STATE (CODE REALITY)
- Startup banner rendering lives in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs::render_startup_banner()`.
- It already prints a strong first draft of the full experience:
  - ASCII art logo + subtitle
  - Server Configuration table
  - Database Statistics table
  - Web UI box
  - Stats Showcase JSON-ish box
  - Closing rule
- However it is still hand-built with:
  - raw ANSI escape code constants
  - manual width math / ad-hoc unicode width guesses
  - ad-hoc JSON token coloring

GOALS
- Keep rich banner enabled by default in real TTYs when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Zero ANSI spam in non-TTY contexts: fall back to a single plain startup line (grep-friendly).
- Centralize styling via theme (`br-1m6a.12`) and stop scattering `\x1b[` literals through the code.
- Safety by default: all sensitive values must be masked/sanitized using `br-1m6a.18`.
- Determinism: stable output for unit tests/E2E (no time-based animations in the banner).

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION (PREFERRED)
- Render each banner section using real `ftui` widgets (Block/Table/Paragraph/etc) into an offscreen `ftui::Frame` at a fixed width, then export to ANSI via a FrankenTUI exporter (e.g. `ftui-extras` text exporter).
  - This eliminates fragile padding/width bookkeeping.
  - It also allows later upgrades like hyperlinks and theme variants without rewriting string math.
- Transitional allowance: it is fine if `render_startup_banner()` still returns `Vec<String>` as long as those strings are produced from widget export rather than manual ANSI.

REQUIRED SECTIONS
1) Logo header (brand)
- Maintain the ASCII art identity, but generate styling via theme rather than hardcoded CYAN/BLUE/MAG constants.
- Keep subtitle line: "Agent Coordination via Message Passing".

2) Server Configuration
- Show (at minimum):
  - environment
  - endpoint
  - database URL (sanitized)
  - storage root
  - auth enabled
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`
  - `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`
  - `CONSOLE_THEME` (active theme id/name from `br-1m6a.12`, for discoverability)
- Values MUST pass through masking/sanitization helper.

3) Database Statistics
- Existing counts plus `pending_acks` (available in HUD snapshot).

4) Web UI
- Keep the existing Web UI box.
- `br-1m6a.9` upgrades the link set and optional OSC-8 hyperlinks.

5) JSON Stats Showcase
- `br-1m6a.17` upgrades this to real JSON + syntax highlighting.

6) Console Layout Summary (for testability)
- Include a single line describing the active console layout config (from `br-1m6a.19`).
  - E2E must be able to assert the mode without parsing inline/alt-screen control codes.

GATING
- Banner should only render when:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- Otherwise: emit one plain startup line like:
  - `mcp-agent-mail listening on http://HOST:PORT (rich console disabled or non-tty)`

TESTING
- Update/extend existing `console.rs` banner tests to assert stable headers/sections and that secrets are redacted.
- Tests should use shared ANSI/OSC stripping helpers from `br-1m6a.14`.

NOTES
- `br-1m6a.10` remains the focused regression bead ensuring config values are masked in the banner.","notes":"STRETCH (banner polish + FrankenTUI showcase): Console capabilities + first-run discoverability

- Add a compact `Console Capabilities` box/row in the banner that surfaces what FrankenTUI detected and what features are active:
  - truecolor / hyperlinks (OSC-8) / synchronized output / mouse
  - active `CONSOLE_THEME`
  - active layout mode summary (`inline bottom 33%` etc)
- Keep at least one plain-text line (`ConsoleCaps: ...`) so `br-1m6a.15` can assert it without parsing control codes.
- If capabilities are unknown/unreliable, prefer conservative wording and allow env overrides to disable features (e.g., force hyperlinks off).

Goal: users instantly understand why hyperlinks/animations are or aren’t showing up.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.053888754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252388170Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.1","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.053888754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-1m6a.10","title":"Mask sensitive values in startup banner config display","description":"Apply the shared masking/sanitization utility (`br-1m6a.18`) to the startup banner so secrets cannot leak.

CURRENT STATE
- Banner rendering lives in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (`render_startup_banner`).
- It prints config-ish fields (endpoint, database_url, storage_root, auth/tool logging flags).
- It currently does NOT guarantee secrets are redacted correctly.

REQUIREMENTS
- All potentially sensitive config values rendered by the banner must be passed through the shared helper(s) from `br-1m6a.18`.
- Explicit allowlist exceptions must be respected:
  - `project_key` MUST remain visible (never masked).
  - `storage_root` MUST remain visible.

WHAT TO MASK/SANITIZE IN THE BANNER
- Anything that is an actual secret:
  - bearer tokens, JWT secrets, API keys, passwords.
- Non-obvious secret carriers:
  - `database_url` (may embed `user:pass@`): sanitize credentials in-place (preserve host/db name).
  - redis URLs (if/when displayed): same.

IMPLEMENTATION
- In the "Server Configuration" section of the banner:
  - Use `sanitize_known_value(key, value)` first.
  - If key is sensitive, replace with `<redacted>`.
  - Otherwise keep the value.
- If the banner includes any JSON blob (stats showcase) that contains sensitive keys, run `mask_json()` before pretty-printing.

TESTING (REGRESSION)
- Provide a banner render test that injects a fake secret (e.g. `bearer_token = "secret123"`) and asserts:
  - Output contains `<redacted>`
  - Output does NOT contain `secret123`
- Add a test proving `project_key` stays visible when present in JSON args.
- Add a test for `database_url` credential stripping: `postgres://u:p@h/db` -> `postgres://u:<redacted>@h/db` (exact format TBD, but password must not appear).

DONE WHEN
- Startup banner cannot leak secrets, even if config values contain embedded credentials.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.447222821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.259349320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.447222821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:49.979527948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:55.240437818Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","author":"CobaltTower","text":"Reopened: Closed prematurely; masking helper + banner integration not implemented yet.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:26:44Z"}]}

+{"id":"br-1m6a.12","title":"Centralized theme system with adaptive colors","description":"Create a centralized semantic theme/palette for *all* console output (banner, tool panels, request panels, HUD), using FrankenTUI's built-in theme system as the single source of truth.

WHY
- We want the console to feel cohesive, branded, and polished (not a pile of ad-hoc ANSI constants).
- FrankenTUI already ships a strong theme system (multiple curated palettes + cached semantic styles + syntax theme integration). We should leverage it rather than reinventing a parallel palette.

CURRENT STATE
- Rich console output is split across:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (banner + tool panels + sparkline + toast/log panel helpers)
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` (HTTP request panel + HUD rendering)
- `console.rs` still uses hardcoded raw ANSI escape code strings (CYAN_B, BLUE, MAG, etc.).

SOURCE OF TRUTH (DO NOT REINVENT)
- Use `ftui_extras::theme`:
  - `ThemeId`, `set_theme`, `current_theme_name`, `current_palette`
  - `semantic_styles_cached()` for consistent `Style` tokens
  - `syntax_theme()` (feature `syntax`) so JSON/tool panels match the active palette

CONFIG (ENABLED BY DEFAULT IN REAL TTYS)
- Add env var `CONSOLE_THEME` (string):
  - Values: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`
  - Default: `cyberpunk_aurora`
- This is console-only (must not affect server semantics).
- Persist `CONSOLE_THEME` alongside other `CONSOLE_*` keys in the user envfile from `br-1m6a.19` so interactive tuning is remembered.

INTEGRATION
1) Startup wiring
- When rich console is active (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` + TTY), set the theme early (before banner/HUD render) by calling `ftui_extras::theme::set_theme(...)`.

2) Replace ad-hoc styling
- Replace hardcoded ANSI constants and scattered color decisions with semantic styles:
  - success/warn/error/info/muted/title/header/link
- When rendering via `Frame` + exporter, rely on `Style` tokens rather than emitting raw SGR.

3) Syntax highlighting alignment
- Any use of `ftui_extras::syntax::SyntaxHighlighter` should be constructed with the active `syntax_theme()` so JSON looks coherent across banner/tool panels.

NON-TTY / RICH DISABLED
- If `stdout.is_terminal()==false` or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false`: do not emit rich panels; keep output plain and grep-friendly.

TESTING
- Unit tests for parsing `CONSOLE_THEME` -> `ThemeId` mapping.
- Render/unit tests must use `ftui_extras::theme::ScopedThemeLock` to avoid cross-test theme races.
- Regression test: after migration, no raw `\x1b[` literals remain in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` except inside exporter/normalizer helpers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.649220290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:38:10.015301954Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:38:10.015215022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.12","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.649220290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-1m6a.13","title":"Enhanced request log panel matching Python parity","description":"Enhance `render_http_request_panel()` to be theme-consistent and parity-aligned with the legacy Python request logging UX.

CURRENT STATE (ALREADY IMPLEMENTED)
- `LOG_JSON_ENABLED` exists in `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` and is already honored.
  - When enabled: emit JSON line to stderr via `http_request_log_json_line()`.
  - When disabled: emit KV/structlog-like line.
- `render_http_request_panel()` exists and emits a 3-line ASCII panel.

WHAT'S MISSING
- Better method/status badge coloring (2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx + GET/POST/DELETE/etc).
- Theme unification (stop hardcoding ANSI colors inside this function).
- Respect rich-output gates so we don't dump ANSI art in non-TTY contexts.

TARGET STATE
1) Status + method styles (from theme `br-1m6a.12`)
- `fn status_style(code: u16) -> Style`
  - 2xx: SUCCESS, bold
  - 3xx: INFO
  - 4xx: WARNING, bold
  - 5xx: DANGER, bold
- `fn method_style(method: &str) -> Style`
  - GET: INFO
  - POST: PRIMARY
  - PUT/PATCH: ACCENT
  - DELETE: DANGER

2) Panel layout
- Title: `{METHOD}  {PATH}  {STATUS}  {MS}ms` with per-segment styles.
- Body: `client: {ip}` in muted style.
- Border: rounded + muted, width capped (keep deterministic, default 100).

3) Gating
- Only render the rich-ish panel when:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- Otherwise fall back to the existing plain-text fallback line.

TESTING
- Unit tests for style mapping:
  - status: 200, 301, 404, 500
  - method: GET, POST, DELETE
- Unit test that JSON stderr emission stays valid JSON.
- Unit tests that non-TTY or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false` results in fallback (no ANSI panel).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.753047760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:41:26.675634112Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.13","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.753047760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.13","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:15:43.613416111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-1m6a.14","title":"Comprehensive unit tests for console output enhancements","description":"Ensure we have comprehensive, *stable* unit test coverage for all console-output enhancements (banner, tool panels, HUD, request panels, masking, theming, layout persistence).

IMPORTANT
- Console output is inherently brittle; these tests must focus on stable invariants and use robust ANSI/OSC normalization.
- Do NOT wait until the very end: this bead should progress incrementally as features land.

CURRENT STATE
- We already have meaningful unit tests in:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (banner structure, tool panel structure, masking basics, sparkline sampling, toast formatting, log panel rendering)
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` (request panel + JSON emission helpers)
- But several of these tests encode behavior that we now intend to change (e.g. `project_key` masking, truncation defaults, config gating).

TEST HARNESS REQUIREMENTS (FOUNDATIONAL)
1) ANSI/OSC normalization helpers (shared across tests)
- Strip SGR (`\x1b[...m`), cursor movement, and OSC-8 hyperlinks.
- Provide `assert_contains_normalized(haystack, needle)` helpers.

2) Deterministic width handling
- Tests should render at fixed widths (80/100/120) and assert on headers/labels rather than exact spacing.

3) Theme determinism
- Any render test that depends on the active FrankenTUI theme must guard against global theme races using `ftui_extras::theme::ScopedThemeLock`.

COVERAGE CHECKLIST (WHAT MUST BE TESTED)
- Masking/sanitization (`br-1m6a.18`):
  - `project_key` + `storage_root` never masked
  - common secret keys are masked
  - URL credential stripping works
- Theme system (`br-1m6a.12`):
  - `CONSOLE_THEME` parsing and mapping
  - syntax highlighting uses `syntax_theme()` for coherence
- Banner (`br-1m6a.1`, `br-1m6a.9`, `br-1m6a.17`, `br-1m6a.10`):

+{"id":"br-1m6a.18","title":"Sensitive value masking utility for console output","description":"Create a single, shared masking/sanitization utility so *all* console output (banner, tool-call panels, request panels, etc.) is safe by default.

WHY
- We must never render secrets into the terminal (bearer tokens, JWT secrets, API keys, passwords, etc.).
- Multiple console features need this (startup banner config table, tool-call args/result panels).
- Current `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` masking is a good start but is *not correct yet*:
  - It masks `project_key` (bad: it is a core identity signal we want visible).
  - It uses a non-ASCII bullet mask and includes overly-broad patterns like `auth`.

CORE API (SERVER CONSOLE MODULE)
- Keep these helpers in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (or a tiny sibling module imported by it).

1) `fn is_sensitive_key(key: &str) -> bool`
- Case-insensitive key matching.
- MUST have explicit allowlist exceptions first:
  - `project_key` MUST NOT be masked.
  - `storage_root` MUST NOT be masked.
- Sensitive substrings (case-insensitive):
  - `token`, `secret`, `password`, `credential`, `bearer`, `jwt`, `api_key`, `private_key`
- DO NOT treat `auth` as sensitive (too broad; would mask lots of safe boolean/enum fields).

2) `fn mask_sensitive_value(original: &str) -> String`
- Use an ASCII-only placeholder for maximum portability/determinism:
  - Recommended: `<redacted>`
- Keep it constant (do not depend on original length) to avoid width surprises.

3) `fn sanitize_known_value(key: &str, value: &str) -> Option<String>`
- Additional safety net for common non-obvious secret carriers where the *key* is not obviously sensitive:
  - `database_url`: if value looks like `scheme://user:pass@host/...`, mask only the password segment.
  - `*_redis_url` / `redis_url` / `http_rate_limit_redis_url`: same.
- Return `Some(sanitized)` if a transformation is applied; else `None`.
- Keep this conservative; prefer obvious, stable parsing over regex.

4) `fn mask_json(value: &serde_json::Value) -> serde_json::Value`
- Recursively walk objects/arrays.
- If an object key is sensitive: replace the value with the redaction placeholder.
- Else, recurse.
- Also apply `sanitize_known_value()` for known keys.

INTEGRATIONS (CALL SITES)
- Tool call start/end panels (`br-1m6a.2`): mask args *before* pretty-printing; mask result previews too.
- Startup banner config display (`br-1m6a.10`): sanitize DB/redis URLs; mask auth/bearer/JWT secrets.

TESTING (UNIT)
- `is_sensitive_key`:
  - Positive: bearer_token, api_key, password, HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN, jwt_secret, private_key
  - Negative: project_key, storage_root, endpoint, app_environment
  - Case-insensitive behavior
- `sanitize_known_value`:
  - postgres URL with credentials -> password redacted, host preserved
  - sqlite path -> unchanged
- `mask_json` recursion:
  - Nested objects + arrays
  - Empty objects
  - Non-string values (numbers/bools/null) under sensitive keys still redacted
  - `project_key` remains visible (regression)

PERF/SAFETY NOTES
- This runs only when we are about to emit console output.
- Avoid regex on the hot path; keep allocations reasonable.","notes":"Implementation note: is_sensitive_key also exact-matches authorization and auth_header as sensitive (common bearer-token carriers) while still avoiding broad auth substring masking. Tool-call end result previews are masked via JSON parse + mask_json before truncation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:05.403325350Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:27:39.967870031Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:27:39.967783289Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.18","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:05.403325350Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-1m6a.19","title":"Console layout configuration (HUD sizing + persistence)","description":"Make the StartupDashboard console layout user-configurable, interactively adjustable, and persistable.

BACKGROUND
- Today `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` hardcodes inline HUD sizing:
  - `ui_height ~= term_height / 3`
  - `UiAnchor::Bottom`
- Users explicitly want to control how much screen is "static HUD" vs "scrolling logs":
  - bottom 20%, bottom 50%
  - left 30% (requires AltScreen + internal log viewer)

GOALS
- Rich HUD remains enabled by default in real TTYs (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` default).
- Config can be set via env vars and persisted to a user-local config envfile.
- Interactive configuration is available by default for humans in HTTP mode (STDIO mode cannot steal stdin from MCP protocol).
- Persisted settings are automatically remembered (no manual edits required).

SCOPE (PHASED)

PHASE 1 (P0): ENV-DRIVEN LAYOUT CONFIG (NO INTERACTIVE INPUT)
Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.

Inline HUD sizing:
- `console_ui_height_percent: u16` (default 33, clamp 10..80)
- `console_ui_anchor: ConsoleUiAnchor` (default bottom; values: bottom|top)
- `console_ui_auto_size: bool` (default false)
- `console_inline_auto_min_rows: u16` (default 8, clamp >= 4)
- `console_inline_auto_max_rows: u16` (default 18, clamp >= min_rows)

Split mode config (required for "left 30%"):
- `console_split_mode: ConsoleSplitMode` (default `inline`; values: inline|left)
- `console_split_ratio_percent: u16` (default 30, clamp 10..80)

Console theme selection (FrankenTUI)
- `CONSOLE_THEME` is defined in `br-1m6a.12` and must be persisted in the same user envfile so humans can tune the look-and-feel once and have it stick.
  - Values: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`
  - Default: `cyberpunk_aurora`

Env vars:
- `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`
- `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`
- `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`
- `CONSOLE_INLINE_AUTO_MIN_ROWS`
- `CONSOLE_INLINE_AUTO_MAX_ROWS`
- `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE`
- `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT`
- `CONSOLE_THEME`

Server integration (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs`):
- If `console_split_mode=inline`:
  - If `console_ui_auto_size=false`: `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }`
  - Else: `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }`
  - Anchor uses `UiAnchor::{Bottom,Top}`
- If `console_split_mode=left`:
  - Use `ScreenMode::AltScreen` and delegate rendering to `br-1m6a.20` (log viewer + left HUD).

Banner/HUD discoverability:
- Add a single plain-text line describing the active console layout config:
  - e.g. `Console: inline bottom 33%` / `Console: inline_auto top 8..18 rows` / `Console: left 30% (alt-screen)`
  - E2E must assert on this line (no parsing of control sequences).

PHASE 2 (P1): INTERACTIVE CONFIG (HTTP MODE ONLY)
This must be available by default for humans (TTY + HTTP mode), and safe in CI.

GATING
- Only enable interactive config when:
  - HTTP server mode
  - `stdin.is_terminal()==true`
  - `config.log_rich_enabled==true`
- Provide an env override:
  - `CONSOLE_INTERACTIVE=0` disables
  - `CONSOLE_INTERACTIVE=1` forces enable (even if we later default it on)

KEYBINDINGS (MINIMUM)
- `+` / `Up`: increase `console_ui_height_percent` by 5
- `-` / `Down`: decrease by 5
- `t` / `b`: toggle anchor top/bottom
- `a`: toggle inline auto-size
- `[` / `]`: decrease/increase `console_split_ratio_percent` by 5 (when in left split)
- `i`: switch to inline mode
- `l`: switch to left split mode
- `?`: toggle a help overlay (shows current values + keys)

APPLYING CHANGES
- Inline height/anchor changes should apply live.
- Switching `console_split_mode` may require recreating the `TerminalWriter` (drop + re-init). If live switching is too risky initially:
  - persist the new mode and show a toast/log line "Restart required to apply console mode change".

PERSISTENCE (AUTO-REMEMBER SETTINGS)
- Persist to a user-local envfile (NOT repo `.env`):
  - default: `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`
  - override: `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH=/path/to/file`
- Read this envfile at startup so console settings apply automatically:
  - For `CONSOLE_*` keys: precedence `real env > persisted user envfile > defaults`
  - Do NOT fall back to working-directory `.env` for `CONSOLE_*` keys (avoid repo contamination)
- File format: `KEY=value` lines (envfile style) so the existing config loader can ingest it.
- Auto-save by default:
  - `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE` (default true)
  - Save ONLY the console-related keys (including `CONSOLE_THEME`).
  - Preserve unrelated lines/comments when updating an existing file.

TESTING
- Unit tests (core config): parsing, clamping, defaults.
- Unit tests (server): percent->rows mapping for small terminals; anchor mapping.
- Unit tests (persistence): envfile update is idempotent and preserves unrelated content.
- Unit tests (interactive): factor key->state updates into a pure helper so we can test each keybinding without needing raw-mode PTY input.
- E2E (`br-1m6a.15`): start server with layout env vars and assert banner reports active layout.

NOTES
- STDIO mode must never start an input reader thread.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltTower","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:32.023878914Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","updated_at":"2026-02-06T22:57:28.210355885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.19","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:32.023878914Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"br-1m6a.4","title":"Toast notifications for important agent events","description":"Add toast notifications for important agent events, using FrankenTUI's real notification queue widgets.

WHY
- Agent Mail is event-heavy (registering agents, conflicts, releases, ack escalations). A HUD without toasts forces users to scan scrollback.

GOALS
- Toasts are enabled by default when rich console is active in a real TTY.
- Non-HUD / non-TTY contexts must stay grep-friendly: degrade to a plain log line (no ANSI art dumps).
- Keep behavior testable without sleeps.

IMPLEMENTATION
1) Use FrankenTUI queue types
- Add to `StartupDashboard`:
  - `notifications: Mutex<ftui_widgets::notification_queue::NotificationQueue>`
- Queue config (initial):
  - `max_visible`: 3-4
  - `max_queued`: 20
  - position: `ToastPosition::TopRight` (or inside HUD area if we restrict drawing to the HUD rect)

2) API surface
- `fn push_toast(&self, toast: Toast, prio: NotificationPriority)`
- Convenience helpers:
  - `toast_info(msg)`, `toast_warning(msg)`, `toast_error(msg)`

3) Rendering
- In the dashboard render path, render `NotificationStack::new(&queue)` as an overlay.
  - If overlaying over the whole terminal is not safe in Inline mode, render it inside the HUD area only.

4) Non-HUD degradation
- If no dashboard is active (stdio mode or non-TTY), toast events become plain one-line logs.
  - Prefer `console_info/console_warning/console_error` once `br-1m6a.5` exists.

EVENT HOOKS (INITIAL SET)

  - `file_reservation_paths`: conflict -> warning toast with conflict summary
  - `force_release_file_reservation` success -> warning toast
  - repeated server errors: if >5 errors in 10s -> error toast
  - messaging bursts: if >20 send/reply in 5s -> info toast

ACTIONS (OPTIONAL, NICE-TO-HAVE)
- For some toasts, include actions:
  - "Copy endpoint" (copies URL to clipboard if available; otherwise just logs)
  - "Open web UI" (only if we ever support shell-out; otherwise omit)

TESTING
- Unit tests for queue behavior (no sleeps):
  - push respects max_queued
  - urgent priority jumps ahead
  - dismiss/dismiss_all behavior
  - auto-expiry can be simulated by mutating `toast.state.created_at` to an old instant, then calling `queue.tick(...)` (no wall-clock sleeps)

- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.

- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# e2e_console.sh - PTY/TTY-focused E2E suite for rich console output.
+#
+# Run via:
+#   ./scripts/e2e_test.sh console
+#
+# This suite validates that rich console output is enabled by default in real
+# terminals, and that envfile-persisted `CONSOLE_*` settings are loaded.
+#
+# Artifacts:
+#   tests/artifacts/console/<timestamp>/*
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Safety: default to keeping temp dirs so the shared harness doesn't run `rm -rf`.
+: "${AM_E2E_KEEP_TMP:=1}"
+
+E2E_SUITE="${E2E_SUITE:-console}"
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+# shellcheck source=./e2e_lib.sh
+source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/e2e_lib.sh"
+
+e2e_init_artifacts
+e2e_banner "Console (PTY) E2E Test Suite"
+
+e2e_save_artifact "env_dump.txt" "$(e2e_dump_env 2>&1)"
+
+for cmd in script timeout python3; do
+    if ! command -v "${cmd}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        e2e_log "${cmd} not found; skipping suite"
+        e2e_skip "${cmd} required"
+        e2e_summary
+        exit 0
+    fi
+done
+
+e2e_fatal() {
+    local msg="$1"
+    e2e_fail "${msg}"
+    e2e_summary || true
+    exit 1
+}
+
+pick_port() {
+python3 - <<'PY'
+import socket
+s = socket.socket()
+s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
+print(s.getsockname()[1])
+s.close()
+PY
+}
+
+normalize_transcript() {
+    local in_path="$1"
+    local out_path="$2"
+    python3 - <<'PY' "$in_path" "$out_path"
+import re
+import sys
+
+in_path = sys.argv[1]
+out_path = sys.argv[2]
+
+data = open(in_path, "rb").read()
+
+# Strip OSC sequences (BEL or ST terminator).
+data = re.sub(rb"\x1b\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\)", b"", data)
+# Strip CSI sequences (colors + cursor movement).
+data = re.sub(rb"\x1b\[[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]", b"", data)
+# Strip single-character ESC sequences (best-effort).
+data = re.sub(rb"\x1b[@-_]", b"", data)
+
+text = data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+
+# Remove util-linux `script` wrapper lines for stable assertions.
+lines = []
+for line in text.splitlines():
+    if line.startswith("Script started on "):
+        continue
+    if line.startswith("Script done on "):
+        continue
+    lines.append(line)
+text = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
+
+with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+    f.write(text)
+PY
+}
+
+start_server_pty() {
+    local label="$1"
+    local port="$2"
+    local db_path="$3"
+    local storage_root="$4"
+    local bin="$5"
+    shift 5
+
+    local typescript="${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR}/server_${label}.typescript"
+    e2e_log "Starting PTY server (${label}): 127.0.0.1:${port}"
+    e2e_log "  typescript: ${typescript}"
+
+    local timeout_s="${AM_E2E_SERVER_TIMEOUT_S:-15}"
+
+    # Run the server in a PTY so stdout/stderr are treated as a real terminal.
+    # Use `timeout` to guarantee the process eventually exits even if a test fails.
+    (
+        script -q -f -c "env \
+DATABASE_URL=sqlite:////${db_path} \
+STORAGE_ROOT=${storage_root} \
+HTTP_HOST=127.0.0.1 \
+HTTP_PORT=${port} \
+HTTP_RBAC_ENABLED=0 \
+HTTP_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=0 \
+HTTP_JWT_ENABLED=0 \
+HTTP_ALLOW_LOCALHOST_UNAUTHENTICATED=0 \
+${*} \
+timeout ${timeout_s}s ${bin} serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port ${port}" \
+            "${typescript}"
+    ) >/dev/null 2>&1 &
+
+    echo $!
+}
+
+stop_server_pty() {
+    local pid="$1"
+    if kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
+        kill "${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
+        sleep 0.2
+        kill -9 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
+    fi
+}
+
+BIN="$(e2e_ensure_binary "mcp-agent-mail" | tail -n 1)"
+
+e2e_case_banner "default_rich_console_enabled (LOG_RICH_ENABLED unset)"
+WORK1="$(e2e_mktemp "e2e_console_default_rich")"
+DB1="${WORK1}/db.sqlite3"
+STORAGE1="${WORK1}/storage"
+mkdir -p "${STORAGE1}"
+PORT1="$(pick_port)"
+
+PID1="$(start_server_pty "default_rich" "${PORT1}" "${DB1}" "${STORAGE1}" "${BIN}")"
+if ! e2e_wait_port 127.0.0.1 "${PORT1}" 10; then
+    stop_server_pty "${PID1}"
+    e2e_fatal "server failed to start (port not open)"
+fi
+sleep 0.6
+stop_server_pty "${PID1}"
+sleep 0.3
+
+NORM1="${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR}/server_default_rich.normalized.txt"
+normalize_transcript "${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR}/server_default_rich.typescript" "${NORM1}"
+OUT1="$(cat "${NORM1}")"
+e2e_assert_contains "banner includes Server Configuration" "${OUT1}" "Server Configuration"
+e2e_assert_contains "banner includes Database Statistics" "${OUT1}" "Database Statistics"
+e2e_assert_contains "banner includes Web UI" "${OUT1}" "Web UI"
+
+e2e_case_banner "banner_suppressed_when_rich_disabled"
+WORK2="$(e2e_mktemp "e2e_console_no_rich")"
+DB2="${WORK2}/db.sqlite3"
+STORAGE2="${WORK2}/storage"
+mkdir -p "${STORAGE2}"
+PORT2="$(pick_port)"
+
+PID2="$(start_server_pty "no_rich" "${PORT2}" "${DB2}" "${STORAGE2}" "${BIN}" "LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false")"

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# test_console.sh - E2E test suite wrapper for PTY/TTY console output.
+#
+# Runs the implementation in scripts/e2e_console.sh so the suite can be invoked via:
+#   ./scripts/e2e_test.sh console
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+
+# Safety: default to keeping temp dirs so shared harness cleanup doesn't run `rm -rf`.
+: "${AM_E2E_KEEP_TMP:=1}"
+
+bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../../scripts/e2e_console.sh"
